learning_outcomes:
1. Major ProgrammeAt the end of the
programme, students should be able to: K  |  Knowledge
outcomes
K.1  |  Describe the
fundamental concepts in community health
K.2  |  Explain the
theoretical models and frameworks related to health promotion
K.3  |  Describe the
roles of individuals and families with self-care and self-management skills,
and the community in diseases prevention and health promotion
S  |  Skill outcomes
S.1  |  Assess
individual and community needs for health promotion
S.2  |  Plan effective
health promotion programme
S.3  |  Implement health
promotion plan
S.4  |  Conduct
evaluation and research related to health promotion
S.5  |  Administer and
manage health promotion programme
S.6  |  Serve as a
health promotion resource person
V  |  Value outcomes
V.1  |  Demonstrate
respect for cultural diversity and sensitivity
V.2  |  Demonstrate
dedication to sustainable development
V.3  |  Demonstrate care
for social equity and justice
study_scheme:
Community Health Practice Community Health Practice Applicable to students admitted in 2025-26
Major Programme Requirement (for
Associate Degree or Higher Diploma holders admitted to senior-year places)
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 51 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required
Courses:
CHPR3001, 3002, 3008, 4001, 4002,
4003, 4004, 4005, 4008, 4901[a], 4902[a], GRON3211#, PHPC1001#, 1012#, 1017#, 2005#, 2017#  |  45
2.  |  Elective
Courses:
6 units of
courses chosen from the following, with a maximum of 1 course from Group
B: Group A: CHPR4011, 4012, PHPC2007#,
2009#, 2020#, 3010#, 3018#,
3034#, 4025#,
4032#, 4033#, 4040#, 4041#, 4042#, 4043# Group B: ANTH4330#, GPAD3255#, SOCI3227#,
SPED2540#, 4560#, 4570#  |  6
Total:  |  51
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  CHPR courses at 2000 and above level as well as those labeled as # will be included in the
calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
